caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the work required per unit mass of gas liquefied. The screen displays a slide titled "Tutorial - 2" with a detailed thermodynamic diagram showing various processes such as constant temperature (T=const), constant pressure (p=const), and constant enthalpy (h=const) paths. The slide includes calculations for the work done, with values like \( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}} = 463 \) and \( y = 0.074 \). These values are used to determine the work per unit mass of gas liquefied, resulting in \( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}_f} = 6265.22 \, J/g \). The cursor moves across the screen, highlighting different parts of the text and diagram, likely explaining the concepts and calculations step-by-step. The presentation is by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, as indicated at the bottom of the slide.
